speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Let's start by checking your vitals — your systolic blood pressure today is 144 mmHg.

speaker: patient
text: Thanks, Doctor. I’ve been feeling a bit off lately — especially with my eyes.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve had blurred vision since yesterday morning, and it hasn’t improved.

speaker: doctor
text: Blurred vision can be concerning — let’s check your blood glucose and other labs. Are you taking your medications as prescribed?

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I take Atorvastatin every night, just like you told me.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 74 mmHg — that’s within acceptable range for now.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also been really thirsty and urinating more than usual.

speaker: doctor
text: Your blood glucose is elevated at 12.7 mmol/L — that’s well above target and likely contributing to your symptoms.

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your labs, imaging, and eGFR, I’m diagnosing you with Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3.

speaker: doctor
text: You’re currently on Atorvastatin 40mg nightly — we’ll continue that for now to manage cardiovascular risk.

speaker: doctor
text: Wait — I just rechecked your blood pressure: systolic is now 161 mmHg. That’s higher than the reading we got earlier.

speaker: doctor
text: And your glucose has risen further — now 15.0 mmol/L — which reinforces the need for urgent glycemic control.

speaker: patient
text: That’s strange — I didn’t eat anything between readings. Could stress be affecting it?

speaker: doctor
text: Stress and acute illness can elevate both BP and glucose. Given your CKD and hypertension, we need tighter control.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m going to add Lisinopril 20mg daily — it will help protect your kidneys and lower your blood pressure.

speaker: patient
text: Will that interact with my Atorvastatin? And how soon should I expect improvement in my vision?

speaker: doctor
text: No significant interaction — but we’ll monitor your potassium and creatinine closely. Vision may improve once glucose stabilizes.

speaker: patient
text: Okay. I’ll watch for dizziness — my mom said low BP can cause blurry vision too.

speaker: doctor
text: You’re right to be cautious — your diastolic pressure just dropped to 62 mmHg, which is on the lower side.

speaker: patient
text: Actually, when I checked at home this morning, my blood sugar was the same as that first reading — 12.7 mmol/L — and my systolic was also 144, just like your initial measurement.
